user_query:
As a local guide in Lezhë County, I’m preparing a data-driven shortlist of attractions to recommend by tour type. Please build a small, runnable script that parses the attractions and tags from input/attractions.html, merges them with visit feedback in input/visits.csv, classifies each attraction into categories using input/mapping.yaml, computes per-attraction metrics, and outputs ranked results.

Requirements:
1) Parse attractions from input/attractions.html, extracting:
   - name (string)
   - municipality (string)
   - tags (comma-separated list from the HTML)

2) Classify each attraction into one or more categories by mapping its tags via input/mapping.yaml (category_mapping). If a tag in the HTML is not present in the mapping, ignore it for classification but log it (see quality checks). Deduplicate categories per attraction.

3) Join with input/visits.csv by attraction name. Only include attractions that exist in input/attractions.html when computing aggregates and scores. Any visits that reference an attraction missing from the HTML must be ignored for aggregates and reported in quality checks.

4) For each included attraction, compute:
   - visit_count = number of rows in visits.csv for that attraction
   - total_group_size = sum of group_size for that attraction
   - average_rating = mean of rating for that attraction (round to 2 decimals in the CSV)
   - max_total_group_size = maximum total_group_size across included attractions (used for normalization)
   - score = 0.7 * (average_rating / 5) + 0.3 * (total_group_size / max_total_group_size); round score to 3 decimals in outputs

5) Produce outputs at these exact paths:
   - output/attraction_scores.csv with columns (in this order): attraction, municipality, categories, average_rating, visit_count, total_group_size, score
     • categories should be a semicolon-separated list of category labels for that attraction, sorted alphabetically
     • average_rating rounded to 2 decimals; score rounded to 3 decimals
   - output/top_by_category.json: a JSON object where each key is a category from the mapping and each value is an ordered list (highest first) of up to the top 3 attractions that include that category, each item an object: {"attraction": "<name>", "score": <number rounded to 3 decimals>}
     • Ranking ties must be broken by: (1) higher visit_count, then (2) attraction name ascending
   - output/quality_checks.txt listing one issue per line for:
     • each attraction present in input/visits.csv but not in input/attractions.html, as: Missing attraction in attractions.html: <name>
     • each tag present in the HTML but absent from the mapping, as: Unmapped tag in attractions.html: <tag> (attraction: <name>)

6) Reproducibility: Provide the script so I can generate all outputs by running a single command. Please place it at scripts/score_attractions.py and make it executable with:
   python scripts/score_attractions.py input/attractions.html input/visits.csv input/mapping.yaml output/

Notes:
- Treat only attractions present in input/attractions.html as valid for aggregates and normalization (max_total_group_size should be computed over included attractions only).
- Keep all processing deterministic and based solely on the provided files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
